_comment: Deploy-coverage manifest for edge functions whose absence in the
deployed runtime causes a user-visible failure.
MOTIVATION (MT-009 Test 1 incident, 2026-05-18):
src/lib/match-named-contacts.ts called
supabase.functions.invoke('match-named-contacts-assign')
The directory existed under supabase/functions/, so the existing
check-edge-function-paths.mjs source-drift guard passed. However,
the function was not deployed to production, so Daniel saw a save
failure at runtime.
RULES (enforced by scripts/check-edge-function-deploy-coverage.mjs):
1. Every function name invoked from src/ MUST appear in 'required'.
2. Every name in 'required' MUST have a matching supabase/functions/<name>/ directory.
3. New invoke call sites added in src/ without a matching entry here
fail the prebuild — forcing the author to acknowledge the deploy.
HOW TO ADD A FUNCTION:
• Create supabase/functions/<name>/index.ts (Lovable auto-deploys backend changes).
• Add the name to 'required' below.
• Mention it under the 'Edge functions requiring deploy' section in RELEASE_GATE.md.
WHAT THIS DOES NOT CHECK:
• Live production deploy state. Source-level guard only — CI does
not have prod access. Backend deploy is automatic on Lovable, but
this manifest documents intent + catches forgotten release-note items.
exempt_invokes._comment_exempt: Dynamic / templated invoke names that cannot be statically resolved.
